question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/accounts/{ownerId}/hooks with no hook.id filter (triggering lowerBound=0, upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE) causing a full table scan on the hook table via findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween, exhausting DB CPU and I/O across all mirror node replicas and shutting down ≥30% of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call GET /api/v1/accounts/{ownerId}/hooks with lowerBound=0 and upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE at high concurrency, causing sustained full-range scans via findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween that exhaust the shared PostgreSQL connection pool and degrade ≥30% of mirror node processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with hook.id=gte:0 and hook.id=lte:9223372036854775807 (Long.MAX_VALUE) causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween to perform a maximum-range scan with limit=100, and by repeating this across many concurrent connections, saturate DB I/O and cause ≥30% of mirror node instances to become unresponsive?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all valid ownerId values (numeric entity IDs are sequential and public) and then issue maximum-range findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween queries for each owner concurrently, causing aggregate DB load that shuts down ≥30% of processing nodes without any rate limiting enforced at the repository layer?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any rate limiting on GET /api/v1/accounts/{ownerId}/hooks to issue thousands of findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween queries per second from a single IP, exhausting the Spring thread pool and DB connection pool simultaneously, causing ≥30% of mirror node replicas to stop processing requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user send hook.id=gte:0 with order=desc and limit=100, causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween to perform a reverse full-range scan that is more expensive than a forward scan (no covering index for DESC order), and by repeating this concurrently, cause ≥30% of processing nodes to become unresponsive due to DB sort pressure?
